title: Implement human requirement review, ambiguity, and immutable versions

environment.os: Windows local; GitHub Actions Ubuntu and Windows

commands.name: Missing human-review builder
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-review.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-review.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: All five examples and properties failed because buildRequirementReviewVersion did not exist

commands.name: Canonical-value property counterexample
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-review.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-review.property.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: PROP-10 shrank leading whitespace to the permanent counterexample plainLanguage=' !'; the assertion compared submitted text instead of the canonical stored value

commands.name: Human-review examples and seeded invariants
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-review.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-review.property.test.ts

commands.name: Missing review service and repository
commands.expectedFailure: Both service scenarios failed because InMemoryRequirementReviewRepository did not exist

commands.name: Permissioned service, conflict, and PostgreSQL append proof
Concurrent in-memory reviews forked one source version
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/requirement-review.test.ts -t 'serializes competing'
commands.expectedFailure: Two simultaneous human decisions both appended version 2 from the same proposal because the fixture repository did not serialize its latest-version check

commands.name: Serialized competing in-memory decisions
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/requirement-review.test.ts -t 'serializes competing'

commands.name: Missing review-lineage migration
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/domain-migrations.test.ts -t 'loads the ordered PostgreSQL schema migrations'
commands.expectedFailure: The migration inventory contained 0001 through 0007 while the new test required 0008

commands.name: PostgreSQL latest-source and immutable-lineage guard
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/domain-migrations.test.ts -t 'requires immutable, latest-source lineage'

commands.name: Missing signed review route
commands.expectedFailure: The route suite could not import the not-yet-created requirement review endpoint

commands.name: Signed confirmation, read history, permission denial, and stale conflict routes
Undefined requirement-review browser behavior
commands.expectedFailure: All shared agreement setup passed while three AGR-03 scenarios and fourteen review steps remained undefined

commands.name: Confirm, ambiguity, rejection, and version-history browser journeys
Existing agreement-intake browser contracts exposed editor compatibility regressions
commands.expectedFailure: The new editable fields made one AGR-02 read-only-field locator ambiguous, and the refusal empty state no longer included the prior explicit no-proposal-stored contract

commands.name: Focused production proposal stories after compatibility fix
Source-bound optimized-production browser capture
commands.command: PACTWIRE_CAPTURE_CURATED_EVIDENCE=1 PACTWIRE_EVIDENCE_TASK=AGR-03 PACTWIRE_BDD_PRODUCTION=1 PACTWIRE_BDD_TAGS=@AGR-03 pnpm test:bdd -- --production

commands.name: Unreferenced curated screenshots
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence harness found all four newly captured screenshots before the AGR-03 manifest referenced them

commands.name: Manifest-linked focused acceptance and evidence reconciliation
commands.command: pnpm evidence:check; pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/requirement-review.test.ts --project property tests/property/requirement-review.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/requirement-review.test.ts apps/web/tests/integration/requirement-review-routes.test.ts tests/integration/domain-migrations.test.ts tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts

commands.name: Clean-checkout verification on Ubuntu and Windows at the implementation source commit

results.counterexample: plainLanguage=' !' is retained as an example regression proving change history records the canonical stored value

scenarios.scenario: The privacy officer edits and confirms a source-bound test rule
An unclear proposal remains visibly ambiguous and non-executable
A rejected proposal remains in immutable history

assets.altText: Desktop human requirement review showing a confirmed executable test rule beside its exact fictional agreement quote, required rationale, reviewer identity, decision time, recorded changes, and two-version immutable history.
assets.caption: A named fictional reviewer confirms an edited observable rule; the screen directly states that confirmation is neither legal interpretation nor software approval.

assets.altText: Desktop immutable requirement history showing human-confirmed version 2 linked to preserved non-executable model proposal version 1, with actor, time, rationale, and change count.
assets.caption: The focused history capture proves that the human decision appends a version while retaining the original source-bound model draft.

assets.altText: Narrow human requirement review showing an ambiguous, not-executable decision with the exact fictional source, structured fields, required rationale, reviewer identity, and preserved two-version history.
assets.caption: An unclear recipient restriction remains visibly non-executable and records why a person must clarify it.

assets.altText: Desktop human requirement review showing a rejected, not-executable model draft beside its exact fictional source, human rationale, reviewer identity, decision time, and preserved immutable history.
assets.caption: Rejection cannot create a runnable rule and does not erase the original model proposal or its citation.

proofs.description: Confirmation, edit history, ambiguity, rejection, model-actor rejection, and canonical-value examples
Seeded PROP-10 append-only source preservation and PROP-17 executable-boundary invariants
Permissioned service, audit, stale-source conflict, PostgreSQL transaction, and immutable history proof
Signed confirmation, history, reviewer denial, and bounded stale-review HTTP conflict
Human confirmation, ambiguity, rejection, exact source, and immutable history journeys
Playwright source/rule assertions, non-executable decisions, lineage, and visual capture
Human-only review builder, services, errors, in-memory repository, and PostgreSQL repository
Latest-source lineage, human actor, payload/column consistency, and executable predicate constraints
Source-beside-rule editor, direct authority boundary, decision states, and immutable history
Signed no-store history and human review boundary

knownLimitations: FR-015 agreement-version comparison remains explicitly deferred until its changed-span and affected-journey scenario and evidence pass.
A human confirmation creates a bounded executable test rule; it does not establish legal meaning, safety, compliance, or software approval.
The observable predicate records human-reviewed fields, while deterministic finding evaluation is owned by DET-03.
The browser fixture uses ephemeral in-memory storage; PostgreSQL persistence is exercised separately in integration tests.
